# Security Policy

**Project:** `UniversalSearch-Bulk-Term-Web-App`
**Owner:** `chirag127`
**URL:** `https://github.com/chirag127/UniversalSearch-Bulk-Term-Web-App`

## Vulnerability Reporting

We take the security of our project very seriously. If you believe you have found a security vulnerability, please report it to us promptly so we can address it. We appreciate your help in keeping `UniversalSearch-Bulk-Term-Web-App` secure.

## Reporting Process

1.  **DO NOT** publicly disclose the vulnerability. This includes posting on GitHub Issues, forums, or social media.
2.  **DO NOT** attempt to exploit the vulnerability beyond what is necessary to confirm its existence.
3.  **DO NOT** violate the privacy of other users or disrupt our services.
4.  **DO NOT** scan or probe our infrastructure without prior authorization.

## How to Report

To report a security vulnerability, please send an email to `security@example.com` (replace with actual security contact if available, otherwise use a placeholder or indicate preferred method). Please include the following information in your email:

*   **Subject:** Security Vulnerability Report: [Project Name]
*   A clear description of the vulnerability.
*   Steps to reproduce the vulnerability.
*   The environment in which you discovered the vulnerability (e.g., browser version, operating system, Node.js version, specific dependency versions if applicable).
*   Any proof-of-concept code or data that demonstrates the vulnerability.
*   Your contact information so we can follow up with you.

## Our Commitment

*   We will acknowledge receipt of your report within **48 hours**.
*   We will promptly investigate the reported vulnerability.
*   We will notify you once the vulnerability has been fixed or addressed.
*   We will provide appropriate credit for responsible disclosure.

## Supported Versions & Patching

This project, `UniversalSearch-Bulk-Term-Web-App`, adheres to the latest security practices for its stack (JavaScript, Web Application). We aim to keep all direct dependencies up-to-date. For security issues in third-party dependencies, we will update them in accordance with standard dependency management practices and release schedules.

## Legal Disclaimer

This policy is not a contract and does not create any legally binding obligations. We reserve the right to change this policy at any time. Your participation in security reporting is voluntary.
